tasks.name: Save the highest-paying youth-focused grant open in late 2025
tasks.description: Find open grants focused on education or youth that have a minimum award of at least $5,000 and deadlines between May 1 and December 31, 2025, then save the grant with the highest maximum award to your saved grants list.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the "Find Grants" option in the main navigation menu.
Click the "Open Grant Opportunities" section link.
In the filters sidebar, under "Focus Area", select the checkboxes for "Education" and "Youth & Families".
In the "Minimum Award" numeric field, type "5000".
Use the "Deadline" filter to set the start date to "05/01/2025" and the end date to "12/31/2025".
Open the sort dropdown above the results and select "Maximum Award - High to Low".
In the sorted results list, click on the first grant card that appears at the top of the list.
On the grant detail page, click the "Save to My Grants" (or similarly labeled) button to add this grant to your saved grants.

tasks.name: Create an organization profile with specific program areas
tasks.description: Register a new organization account named 'Green Town Neighbors' and complete its profile by setting the organization type to 501(c)(3), the annual budget to $150,000, and selecting exactly the 'Environment' and 'Neighborhoods' program areas before saving.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the "Sign Up" link in the header.
On the sign-up page, select the "Organization account" option (for example, via a radio button).
In the "Organization Name" field, type "Green Town Neighbors".
In the "Email" field, type "contact@greentown.test".
In both the "Password" and "Confirm Password" fields, type "GrantTest123!".
Click the "Create Account" or "Register" button.
On the account dashboard, click the "Organization Profile" option in the main navigation or sidebar.
In the "Organization Type" dropdown, select "501(c)(3) Nonprofit", and in the "Annual Budget" field, type "150000".
In the "Program Areas" multi-select, choose exactly two options, "Environment" and "Neighborhoods", and then click the "Save Profile" button.

tasks.name: Start a small neighborhood grant application under $5,000 and save it as a draft
tasks.description: Create an individual applicant account, find neighborhood-focused grants, choose the first grant with a maximum award of $5,000 or less, start an application for it, fill in the basic applicant information fields, and save the application as a draft.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the "Sign Up" link in the header.
On the sign-up page, select the "Individual applicant" account type.
In the "Full Name" field, type "Jordan Rivera", in the "Email" field type "jordan@example.test", and in both the "Password" and "Confirm Password" fields type "ApplyNow456!".
Click the "Create Account" button.
On the applicant dashboard, click the "Find Grants" option.
In the filters, under "Focus Area", select the "Neighborhoods" checkbox.
In the sort dropdown above the results, choose "Maximum Award - Low to High", then click the first grant card in the list whose summary shows a maximum award of $5,000 or less.
On that grant's detail page, click the "Start Application" button.

On the "Applicant Information" step of the application form, enter "Community Garden Launch" in the "Project Title" field, "5000" in the "Requested Amount" field, and "09/01/2025" in the "Project Start Date" field, then click the "Save Draft" or "Save & Exit" button.

tasks.name: Choose the arts grant with the lower match requirement between the first two results
tasks.description: Search for arts and culture grants, open the first two results, compare their required match percentages, and add the grant with the lower match requirement to your application plan.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the "Find Grants" option in the main navigation.
In the filters sidebar, under "Focus Area", select the "Arts & Culture" checkbox.
In the sort dropdown above the results list, select "Deadline - Soonest First".
In the results list, open the first two grant titles in separate tabs (for example, by Ctrl+clicking or using an "Open in new tab" option on each of the first two grants).
Switch to the browser tab for the first grant and scroll until the "Match required" percentage field in the overview or details section is visible.
Switch to the browser tab for the second grant and scroll until its "Match required" percentage field is visible.
Compare the two visible "Match required" percentages and keep active the tab for the grant that displays the lower percentage.
On the page for the grant with the lower match requirement, click the "Add to Application Plan" or "Save to My Grants" button.
If a dialog appears asking for a plan name, type "Arts Priority 2025" in the plan name field and click the "Save" button.

tasks.name: Save a comparison of three mid-sized 2023 health grants
tasks.description: Browse the foundation’s past grants, filter to health-related grants awarded in 2023 between $10,000 and $25,000, select the first three in the list, and save them as a named comparison.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the "Grants" item in the main navigation and then select the "Past Grants" section.
In the filters, set the "Year Awarded" dropdown to "2023".
Under "Focus Area", select the "Health & Wellness" checkbox.
Use the amount range control to set the minimum amount to "10000" and the maximum amount to "25000", then apply the filters if there is an "Apply Filters" button.
In the filtered results list, click the "Compare" checkbox or button for each of the first three grants displayed.
Click the "View Comparison" button or link that appears once grants are selected.
On the comparison page, click the "Save this comparison" button, type "Health 2023 mid-range" into the comparison name field, and click the "Save" button.

tasks.name: Subscribe to monthly email alerts for youth and environment grants only
tasks.description: Sign up for the foundation’s grant email alerts using a specific email address, select Youth & Families and Environment as interest areas, choose monthly frequency, and enable only email (not SMS) as the notification method.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the "Grant Email Alerts" or "Get Updates" link located in the header or footer.
In the "Email address" field on the alerts signup form, type "newsletter@example.test".
In the "Geographic focus" dropdown, select "Entire County".
In the "Interests" or "Areas of Interest" section, select the checkboxes for "Youth & Families" and "Environment".
In the "Email frequency" dropdown, choose "Monthly".
In the "Notification methods" section, leave "Email" selected, click the "SMS/Text" option so that it is not selected, and then click the "Subscribe" or "Sign Up" button.

tasks.name: Submit an eligibility question about a specific grant via the contact form
tasks.description: Use the website’s contact form to send an eligibility question about the 'Community Impact Grant', selecting 'Eligibility Question' as the topic and choosing email as the preferred response method.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the "Contact" or "Help" link in the main navigation or footer.
On the support page, click the "Send us a message" or "Contact us" form link.
In the "Topic" dropdown, select "Eligibility Question".
In the "Grant program" dropdown, select "Community Impact Grant" (or the first option that contains the words "Community Impact").
In the "Your Name" field, type "Alex Kim", and in the "Email" field, type "alex@example.test".

In the main message text area, type a question of at least two sentences describing your project and asking whether it is eligible for the Community Impact Grant, including a planned start date and location.
In the "Preferred response method" section, select the "Email" radio button, then click the "Submit" button on the form.

tasks.name: Set deadline reminder emails 14 days before grant due dates and disable blog emails
tasks.description: Create an individual account, open notification settings, turn on deadline reminder emails 14 days before due dates while keeping grant update notifications, and turn off email notifications for news and blog posts before saving.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the "Sign Up" link in the header.
On the sign-up page, choose the "Individual applicant" account type.

In the "Full Name" field type "Taylor Morgan", in the "Email" field type "taylor@example.test", and in both the "Password" and "Confirm Password" fields type "Deadline789!", then click the "Create Account" button.
From the dashboard, click the "Account Settings" or "Notification Settings" option in the navigation.
In the "Deadline reminders" section, turn on or toggle on the option to receive reminders.
In the "Days before deadline" field or dropdown, enter or select "14".
In the "Grant updates" section, keep the checkboxes or toggles for receiving email or in-site notifications about grant status or updates in the on/selected state.
In the "News and blog updates" section, click to turn off or uncheck the option for email notifications about news or blog posts, then click the "Save Settings" button.
